mnemonics: Asterisk is the 'Key' to the 'House' (memory address). Use it to get the value inside (*ptr) or to get the address to go to (&var).
keyPoints: The dereference operator (*) is used to access the value a pointer points to.
The address-of operator (&) is used to get the memory address of a variable.
A pointer must be initialized with a valid memory address before it can be dereferenced.
commonMistakes: Dereferencing a NULL or uninitialized pointer, which leads to undefined behavior (segmentation fault).
Confusing the pointer itself (the address) with the value it points to (the data).
Using the wrong data type for a pointer, leading to incorrect memory interpretation.
